recipes.title: Ceviche
recipes.description: Peru's national dish: fresh white fish cured in lime juice with red onion, ají, and cilantro, served with sweet potato and corn.

ingredients.name: fresh sea bass or sole fillets

steps: Cut the fish into 2 cm cubes and place in a glass bowl.
Soak red onion slices in ice water for 5 minutes, then drain — this removes harshness.
Season the fish with salt, garlic, and ají, then pour over the lime juice (the leche de tigre).
Toss gently and let cure for 3–5 minutes — the fish should turn opaque on the outside.
Add red onion and cilantro, mixing once.
Plate immediately, spooning leche de tigre over the top.
Serve at once with boiled sweet potato slices and choclo on the side.

recipes.title: Lomo Saltado
recipes.description: Iconic Peruvian Chinese-Creole stir-fry of beef, onions, tomatoes, and ají in soy sauce, served with rice and fries.
steps: Season beef strips with salt and pepper.
Heat oil in a wok over very high heat until almost smoking.
Sear beef in batches for about 1 minute until browned but rare. Remove and set aside.
In the same wok, sauté garlic, then add onion and tomato; stir-fry 2 minutes — they should remain crunchy.
Add ají amarillo paste, soy sauce, and vinegar; toss 30 seconds.
Return beef and any juices, add fried potatoes, and toss everything for 30 seconds.
Sprinkle with cilantro and serve immediately over white rice.

recipes.title: Ají de Gallina
recipes.description: Creamy shredded chicken stew thickened with bread and walnuts, perfumed with ají amarillo — a Limeño classic.
steps: Poach chicken in broth for 20 minutes, reserve broth, and shred meat finely.
Soak bread in evaporated milk until softened.
Sauté onion in oil until golden, add garlic and ají amarillo paste, cook 5 minutes.
Blend the onion mixture with soaked bread, walnuts, and 200 ml broth until smooth.
Return mixture to pot, add shredded chicken, parmesan, and more broth as needed.
Simmer gently 10 minutes until creamy and thickened. Season with salt.
Serve over boiled potato slices, garnished with halved boiled eggs and black olives.

recipes.title: Anticuchos
recipes.description: Peruvian beef-heart skewers marinated in ají panca, vinegar, and cumin, then grilled over charcoal until charred and juicy.
steps: Mix ají panca, vinegar, garlic, cumin, oregano, oil, and salt to make a marinade.
Toss beef heart cubes in the marinade and refrigerate at least 4 hours, ideally overnight.
Soak wooden skewers in water 30 minutes (or use metal skewers).
Thread 4–5 cubes onto each skewer.
Grill over very hot charcoal for 2–3 minutes per side, basting with leftover marinade.
Boil potato slices and choclo until tender.
Serve skewers immediately with potato, choclo, and a side of ají sauce.

recipes.title: Causa
recipes.description: Cold layered Peruvian potato terrine with ají amarillo, lime, and a savory filling of chicken, tuna, or shrimp.
steps: Boil potatoes in their skins until very tender, about 25 minutes. Peel while warm and pass through a ricer.
Knead potato puree with ají amarillo, lime juice, oil, and salt until smooth and bright yellow.
Mix shredded chicken with mayonnaise and a pinch of salt.
Line a loaf pan or ring mold with plastic wrap. Press in a layer of potato.
Add a layer of avocado slices, then a layer of chicken filling, repeating to fill.
Finish with a final potato layer, smoothing the top.
Chill at least 1 hour, unmold, and garnish with boiled egg slices and olives.

recipes.title: Rocoto Relleno
recipes.description: Arequipa specialty: spicy red rocoto peppers stuffed with seasoned beef, baked under melted cheese.
steps: Slice the tops off the rocotos, remove seeds and veins, and reserve the caps.
Boil rocotos in water with sugar and vinegar for 5 minutes, then drain. Repeat 3 times to tame the heat.
Brown beef with onion, garlic, ají panca, and cumin until cooked through.
Stir in raisins, peanuts, and chopped boiled egg, then cool the filling.
Stuff each rocoto with the meat mixture, top with a slice of queso fresco, and replace the caps.
Arrange in a baking dish, pour milk around the base, and bake at 180°C for 25 minutes.
Serve hot with a side of pastel de papa or boiled potatoes.

recipes.title: Pollo a la Brasa
recipes.description: Peruvian rotisserie chicken marinated in soy, vinegar, and spices, roasted until crisp-skinned and juicy.
steps: Whisk soy, vinegar, ají panca, garlic, cumin, oregano, paprika, oil, salt, and pepper into a marinade.
Pat chicken dry, then rub marinade all over and under the skin. Refrigerate at least 6 hours, preferably overnight.
Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F). Place chicken breast-side up on a rack over a roasting pan.
Roast 50–60 minutes, basting once or twice, until skin is deep mahogany and juices run clear.
Internal temperature should reach 75°C in the thickest part of the thigh.
Rest 10 minutes loosely tented with foil.
Carve and serve with French fries, salad, and ají verde sauce.

recipes.title: Papas a la Huancaína
recipes.description: Boiled potatoes blanketed in a creamy ají amarillo and queso fresco sauce — a refreshing Andean appetizer.
steps: Boil potatoes in their skins until tender, then peel and slice into thick rounds.
Sauté garlic in a little oil until fragrant; cool.
Blend queso fresco, ají amarillo, evaporated milk, crackers, oil, garlic, lime juice, and salt until silky.
Adjust consistency with more milk if too thick — it should coat a spoon.
Line plates with lettuce leaves and arrange potato slices on top.
Pour the warm Huancaína sauce generously over the potatoes.
Garnish with halved boiled eggs and black olives. Serve immediately.

recipes.title: Tacu Tacu
recipes.description: Peruvian comfort dish of leftover rice and beans pan-fried into a crispy patty, often topped with a steak and fried egg.

ingredients.name: cooked canary or pinto beans, drained

steps: Sauté onion in oil until golden, then add garlic and ají amarillo paste; cook 3 minutes.
Mash beans into the aderezo with a wooden spoon, season with cumin and salt.
Stir in the cooked rice, mixing until everything is well combined.
Heat more oil in a non-stick skillet and press a portion of the rice-bean mixture into a thick oval patty.
Fry over medium-high heat for 4–5 minutes until a deep golden crust forms.
Carefully flip and crisp the other side. Repeat with remaining mixture.
Top each tacu tacu with a fried egg and serve with fried plantains.

recipes.title: Arroz con Pollo
recipes.description: Peruvian green-tinted rice and chicken cooked together with cilantro, beer, and ají amarillo.
steps: Season chicken pieces with salt and pepper, then brown in oil on all sides. Remove and reserve.
Blend cilantro and spinach with a little broth into a smooth green puree.
In the same pot, sauté onion, garlic, and ají amarillo paste for 4 minutes.
Add cilantro puree and cook 2 minutes until fragrant and oil starts to separate.
Stir in rice to coat, then pour in beer and remaining broth. Add chicken pieces, peas, and carrot.
Bring to a boil, reduce to low, cover, and cook 25 minutes until rice is tender and liquid absorbed.
Rest covered 5 minutes, fluff, and serve with salsa criolla.

recipes.title: Suspiro a la Limeña
recipes.description: Limeño dessert of rich dulce de leche custard topped with cinnamon-port meringue.
steps: Combine condensed milk and evaporated milk in a saucepan over medium-low heat.
Cook, stirring constantly, for about 30 minutes until thick, golden, and dulce-de-leche-like.
Off heat, whisk in egg yolks and vanilla, then return to low heat for 2 minutes to thicken.
Divide manjar blanco among glasses and chill until set.
Make Italian meringue: heat sugar with port to 118°C (soft-ball stage).
Whip egg whites to stiff peaks, then slowly pour in hot syrup while beating until glossy and cool.
Pipe meringue over each suspiro and dust generously with cinnamon.

recipes.title: Picarones
recipes.description: Peruvian sweet potato and squash doughnut rings, deep-fried golden and drizzled with chancaca syrup.
steps: Steep anise seeds in warm water 5 minutes, then strain. Activate yeast in this water with a pinch of sugar.
Combine mashed sweet potato, squash, salt, and yeast water with flour, mixing into a soft, sticky dough.
Cover and let rise 1 hour until doubled.
For the syrup, simmer chancaca with 250 ml water, orange peel, cinnamon, and cloves for 20 minutes until syrupy. Strain.
Heat oil to 180°C. Wet your hands, grab a small portion of dough, and shape into a ring with a hole in the center.
Slide rings into hot oil and fry 2 minutes per side until golden and puffed.
Drain briefly on paper towels and serve hot with chancaca syrup poured over.

recipes.title: Seco de Cordero
recipes.description: Slow-braised lamb in a fragrant cilantro and ají sauce, paired with white rice and canary beans.
steps: Season lamb with salt and pepper. Sear in hot oil in a heavy pot until deeply browned. Remove.
In the same pot, sauté onion, garlic, and both ají pastes for 5 minutes.
Blend cilantro, spinach, and a little broth into a vivid green puree, then add to the pot.
Cook puree until oil starts to separate, about 4 minutes.
Return lamb, pour in beer and broth, bring to a simmer, cover, and braise 90 minutes.
Add potatoes and carrot, simmer 30 more minutes until lamb is fork-tender.
Adjust seasoning, rest 5 minutes, and serve with white rice and canary beans.

recipes.title: Arroz con Mariscos
recipes.description: Peruvian seafood rice loaded with shrimp, squid, and mussels in a vibrant ají amarillo broth.
steps: Sauté onion in oil until golden, add garlic, ají pastes, and cook 3 minutes.
Stir in tomato and bell pepper; cook another 3 minutes until softened.
Add rice, toasting briefly, then pour in white wine and let it evaporate.
Add fish broth, season with salt, and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat, cover, and simmer 12 minutes.
Scatter seafood and peas over the rice, cover, and steam 5–6 more minutes until shrimp are pink and mussels open.
Rest 3 minutes off heat, sprinkle with cilantro, and serve with lime wedges.

recipes.title: Tiradito
recipes.description: Peruvian sashimi-style fish in a vibrant ají leche de tigre — a Nikkei fusion of ceviche and Japanese technique.

ingredients.name: very fresh sea bass or tuna

steps: Slice fish very thinly, like sashimi, against the grain.
Arrange slices in a single layer on a chilled plate.
Blend lime juice, ají amarillo paste, garlic, ginger, soy sauce, salt, and a few cilantro leaves until smooth.
Strain the leche de tigre for an ultra-clean sauce, if desired.
Spoon the sauce evenly over the fish slices just before serving.
Garnish with thinly sliced ají limo and cilantro leaves.
Serve immediately, well chilled.

recipes.title: Sopa Criolla
recipes.description: Comforting Peruvian beef noodle soup with ají panca, milk, and a poached egg, finished with fried bread.
steps: Heat oil and quickly sear beef strips for 1 minute. Remove and reserve.
Sauté onion until golden, add garlic, ají panca, tomato paste, cumin, and oregano; cook 4 minutes.
Pour in beef broth, bring to a boil, and simmer 5 minutes for flavors to meld.
Add pasta and cook 3 minutes until almost tender.
Return beef, then stir in evaporated milk and adjust seasoning.
Crack eggs gently into the simmering broth and poach 3 minutes until whites set.
Ladle into bowls with one egg per portion and float a slice of fried bread on top.
